context: Tests whether the agent identifies no-changed-when issues correctly, produces a fixed playbook with proper changed_when annotations, and documents the correct dry-run command (--check --diff together, not just --check).

checklist.name: All no-changed-when tasks identified
checklist.description: The audit_report.md identifies at least 4 of the 6 command/shell tasks as having no-changed-when issues

checklist.name: Why it matters explained
checklist.description: The report explains why missing changed_when breaks idempotency (always reports changed, breaks handler triggering or change auditing)

checklist.name: Read-only tasks get changed_when: false
checklist.description: In fixed_playbook.yml, tasks that only read state (check if config exists, get version, check disk, verify running, pgrep) have `changed_when: false`

checklist.name: State-changing tasks get explicit changed_when
checklist.description: In fixed_playbook.yml, tasks that may actually change state (migrate, reload-config) have an explicit `changed_when` condition rather than just `changed_when: false`

checklist.name: Dry-run uses --check --diff
checklist.description: The audit_report.md shows the dry-run command as `ansible-playbook --check --diff ...` (both flags present)

checklist.name: No --check without --diff
checklist.description: The report does NOT show `--check` used without `--diff` (the anti-pattern is avoided)

checklist.name: missing mode on file task noted
checklist.description: The audit identifies that the file task creating /opt/myapp is missing a `mode:` attribute

checklist.name: mode added in fixed playbook
checklist.description: The fixed_playbook.yml adds a `mode:` attribute to the file task

checklist.name: ansible-lint run documented
checklist.description: The audit_report.md documents running ansible-lint as part of the audit process
